data.title: Microsoft drives up Copilot AI adoption, lands big deal with Barclays: report
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) has apparently gained ground with its Copilot artificial intelligence assistants, landing major deals with Barclays (NYSE:BCS), among others, according to Bloomberg.

Judson Althoff, Microsoft's chief commercial officer, told employees during a company-wide town hall meeting today, Barclays intends to purchase 100,000 licenses for Copilot AI assistants, the report said, citing sources familiar with the event. Althoff also named Accenture (ACN), Toyota (TM) and Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) as large-scale adopters of the AI assistants.

Microsoft has embedded Copilot, its flagship AI offering, across its products. During Microsoft's third quarter earnings call on April 30, CEO Satya Nadella listed an array Copilot-infused applications and strong adoptions.

"And we are previewing a first-of-its-kind SWE agent capable of asynchronously executing developer tasks," Nadella said. "All up, we now have over 15 million GitHub Copilot users up over 4x year-over-year, and both digital natives like Twilio (TWLO) and enterprises like Cisco (CSCO), HPE (HPE), Skyscanner and Target (TGT) continue to choose GitHub Copilot to equip their developers with AI throughout the entire dev life cycle."

Despite the positive rhetoric around Copilot, the report noted that Microsoft "hasn't disclosed a total customer count or the financial impact of those sales."

data.title: Sempra's Port Arthur Phase 2 approved to export LNG
data.description:
Sempra (NYSE:SRE) said Thursday the U.S. Department of Energy issued a permit allowing its Port Arthur LNG Phase 2 development project in Texas to export of up to ~13.5M metric tons/year of liquefied natural gas to markets in Europe and Asia.

The Phase 2 project is expected to include two liquefaction trains capable of producing ~13M metric tons/year of LNG, which would increase the total liquefaction capacity of the Port Arthur LNG facility from 13M tons/year for Phase 1, which is currently under construction, to as much as 26M tons/year.

Port Arthur LNG Phase 2, which still needs a final investment decision, is expected to export 1.91B cf/day once completed; Phase 1 is expected to begin exporting LNG in 2027.

Phase 2 is the first final LNG export approval under President Trump, who reversed the Biden administration's pause on project approvals.

data.title: Gold futures gain, reversing selloff sparked by court ruling on tariffs
data.description:
Gold futures closed higher Thursday, reversing losses early in the session following reports that the U.S. Court of International Trade deemed most of President Trump's tariffs illegal.

The news initially raised risk appetite and triggered a selloff in gold, but the risk-on sentiment was blunted as the Trump administration appealed the decision, then gold benefitted from buying interest from the dip in price, analysts said.

The assumption that the decision would be challenged, likely elevating it to the Supreme Court, replaced the risk-on sentiment with "uncertainty and confusion - that is safe-haven gold's wheelhouse," Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities said in a note.

Gold also was aided by softer jobs data that showed the number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits rose more than expected last week; if the data is a harbinger of a weakening labor market, the Federal Reserve could cut interest rates more quickly.

Bullion is considered a hedge against uncertainty and inflation, and it tends to perform well in low interest rate environments, since the metal does not generate interest.

The market also will scrutinize Friday's release of U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures, one of the Fed's favored data points.

Front-mnth Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June closed +0.7% to $3,317.10/oz, and front-month June silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.8% to $33.283/oz, snapping back-to-back daily losses for both metals.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Red Robin Gourmet Burgers swings to a profit launching shares 55% higher after-hours
data.description:
Red Robin Gourmet Burgers’ (NASDAQ:RRGB) ambitious plan to turn the business around continues to impress investors as the restaurant chain swung to a profit in the first quarter, doubled adjusted EBITDA from last year, lowered labor costs, and cut its debt by nearly $18M.

The results overshadowed lowered guidance for FY25 and expectations for comparable restaurant revenue to decline by 3%, launching shares more than 55% higher in Thursday’s after-hours trading.

“There is still more work to be done as we continue the comeback journey of this beloved brand and capture the significant opportunity in front of us," CEO David Pace said in a statement.

Total sales for the quarter increased by just 1% to an above-consensus $392.35M. But thanks to a 6.8% increase in net menu pricing, as well as lower labor costs and general expenses, Red Robin (NASDAQ:RRGB) swung to an adjusted profit of $0.20 per share versus a loss of $0.73 in the same quarter last year, beating expectations by $0.69. In addition, adjusted EBITDA improved to $27.9M from $13.4M a year ago.

Expectations for the current quarter were lowered to include a headwind of ~240 basis points due to a 2024 benefit from the change in the loyalty program not recurring in 2025. The company now expects adjusted EBITDA of $13M to $16M.

For FY25 total revenue was lowered to $1.21B to $1.23B from $1.225 to $1.250B previously, versus $1.23B estimates. Other estimates remain unchanged including restaurant level operating profit of 12% to 13%, adjusted EBITDA of $60M to $65M, and capital expenditures of ~$30M.

data.title: LanzaTech appoints Sushmita Koyanagi as CFO
data.description:
• LanzaTech Global (NASDAQ:LNZA) announced on Thursday certain transitions in its executive leadership team in connection with its recent financing and ongoing strategic measures. 
  • The announced leadership changes include:Sushmita Koyanagi appointed Chief Financial Officer, effective June 2, 2025. Amanda Fuisz to assume the role of interim General Counsel, effective June 13, 2025, and Gary Rieschel, a long-time serving Board member, to retire from the board of directors. 
  • Koyanagi succeeds Justin Pugh, who has been serving as LanzaTech’s interim CFO since January 2025 and who will maintain an advisory role with the company to assist in his transition and to provide other related support until June 30, 2025. 
  • The announced leadership changes and role consolidations are anticipated to result in annual cost reductions of approximately $1 million.

data.title: Laser Photonics faces Nasdaq non-compliance; shares down
data.description:
• Laser Photonics (NASDAQ:LASE) received a Nasdaq notice for failing to file its Q1 2025 and 2024 annual reports, both delayed due to its November 2024 asset acquisition from CMS.
  • The company has until June 16, 2025, to submit a compliance plan, with a potential extension through October 13, 2025.
  • LPC is working with auditors to resolve issues tied to CMS’s historical revenue practices and asset valuation.
  • LASE shares fall over 8% post-market on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Ventas prices $500M in senior notes due 2032
data.description:
• Ventas (NYSE:VTR) announced its subsidiary Ventas Realty has priced a $500M offering of 5.100% senior notes due July 15, 2032, at 99.391% of par.
  • The notes, fully guaranteed by Ventas, are expected to close on June 3, 2025.
  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including potential debt repayment.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Solowin Launches offering of 20M Class A shares, 1M warrants
data.description:
• Solowin Holdings (NASDAQ:SWIN) is offering 20M Class A ordinary shares priced at $0.33/share, each paired with a warrant to purchase 0.05 shares.
  • The offering includes 1M total warrants exercisable at $0.363/share for 36 months, with cashless exercise available after one month.
  • Proceeds will support general corporate purposes.
  • SWIN shares rise over 4% post-market on Thursday.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Two Harbors expects to record $199M contingency liability
data.description:
Two Harbors Investment (NYSE:TWO), a mortgage REIT, expects to record a contingency liability and related expense of $198.9M in the month of May related to legal proceedings regarding its former external manager, the company said on Thursday in a filing.

The liability and related expense reflects a $139.8M termination fee that the company believes would have been payable to PRCM Advisors for the termination on the basis of unfair compensation under its management agreement, plus applicable prejudgment interest on the termination fee that would have accrued through May 31, 2025.

The company also said the defense or ultimate resolution of the matters could involve significant monetary costs and could have a significant impact on the company.

The liability relates to Two Harbors' (NYSE:TWO) termination for cause of PRCM as its manager and related complaints filed by PRCM Advisors, Pine River Domestic Management L.P., and Pine River Capital Management L.P.

On March 31, 2025, a magistrate judge recommended that Two Harbors' motion for summary judgment be denied and that the plaintiffs' motion for summary judgment be granted in part. Two Harbors (TWO) objected to the report and recommendation, but the objections were overruled on May 23, 2025. 

The plaintiffs' motion for summary judgment was granted to the extent that the company, Two Harbors (TWO) did not a basis on which to base the management agreement for cause. The rest of the plaintiffs' motion was denied.

data.title: Danaher in partnership with AstraZeneca for diagnostic tests for precision medicine
data.description:
• Danaher (NYSE:DHR) has inked an agreement with AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) to develop diagnostic tests and tools that will help doctors determine which precision medicine treatments are best for individual patients.
  • The first product envisioned for development will use technologies from Leica Biosystems, a Danaher's subsidiary. And the initial focus "will be on digital and computational pathology products and AI-assisted algorithms, to help clinicians better diagnose patients by identifying those who are most likely to benefit from targeted therapies."
  • The partnership will also utilize the new Danaher Centers for Enabling Precision Medicine.
